review_comments:
["Using `variable.id` as the `data:` URI path removes the original filename/extension from the resource. When the carousel service falls back to `openSingleImage` (e.g. image isn’t found in collected sections), `buildSingleImageArgs` derives the displayed name and MIME type from `resource.path`, so this can show an internal id and default to `image/png` (potentially breaking non-PNG rendering). Consider encoding both uniqueness and filename in the URI (e.g. `${variable.id}/${variable.name}` or similar), or otherwise ensure the single-image fallback receives the correct name/mimeType.
```suggestion
		const imageUri = URI.isUri(uri) ? uri : URI.from({ scheme: 'data', path: `${variable.id}/${encodeURIComponent(variable.name)}` });
```", 'Passing `attachment.id` down as the carousel resource basis means the generated `data:` URI may lose the original filename/extension. If the carousel service falls back to opening a single image, it derives the title and MIME type from `resource.path`, so users may see an opaque id and non-PNGs may get the wrong MIME. Consider including both id and name in the URI (or passing name/mimeType through) to keep the fallback correct.

This issue also appears on line 474 of the same file.

See below for a potential fix:
```
		const carouselResourceBase = `${attachment.id}-${basename(attachment.name)}`;
		const clickHandler = async () => {
			if ((resource || imageData) && configurationService.getValue<boolean>(ChatConfiguration.ImageCarouselEnabled)) {
				await this.openInCarousel(carouselResourceBase, imageData, resource);
```', "If the `data:` URI is later adjusted to include the original filename/extension (to preserve correct title/MIME in the single-image fallback), update this assertion to match the new URI format (e.g. include the filename as the last path segment, not just the id).

See below for a potential fix:
```

			const imageId = URI.parse(result[0].images[0].id);
			assert.strictEqual(imageId.scheme, 'data');
			assert.ok(imageId.path === 'img-1' || imageId.path.startsWith('img-1/'));

			assert.deepStrictEqual({
				name: result[0].images[0].name,
				mimeType: result[0].images[0].mimeType,
				data: [...result[0].images[0].data],
			}, {
```", "## Pull request overview

This PR updates how chat image attachments without a backing file URI are assigned a synthetic `data:` URI, switching from using the attachment **name** to using the attachment **id**. This appears intended to avoid collisions when multiple images share the same filename.

**Changes:**
- Generate `data:` URIs for request attachment images using `variable.id` (instead of `variable.name`).
- Open the carousel for image attachments using `attachment.id` (instead of `attachment.name`) when synthesizing a `data:` URI.
- Update carousel service tests to expect the new `data:` URI shape.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/test/browser/chatImageCarouselService.test.ts | Adjusts test expectation for synthesized `data:` URI ids. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatImageExtraction.ts | Changes synthesized `data:` URI creation for request attachment images to use `variable.id`. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/attachments/chatAttachmentWidgets.ts | Changes synthesized `data:` URI creation for carousel opening to use `attachment.id`. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/attachments/chatAttachmentWidgets.ts:476**
* `URI.from({ scheme: 'data', path: id })` drops any filename/extension information. The carousel service’s single-image fallback infers name + MIME from the URI path, so this can regress display and decoding for non-PNG images. Consider constructing a stable-but-descriptive path (e.g. `${id}/${attachment.name}`) so `buildSingleImageArgs` can infer correctly.
```
	private async openInCarousel(id: string, data: Uint8Array | undefined, referenceUri: URI | undefined): Promise<void> {
		const resource = referenceUri ?? URI.from({ scheme: 'data', path: id });
		await this.chatImageCarouselService.openCarouselAtResource(resource, data);
```
</details>


- **Files reviewed:** 3/3 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 3


"]

commits: [{'sha': '97cd6851a31d6f5587a2ac36840999283d58b3ff', 'message': 'Fix Agent Debug Panel flow chart for new JSONL-based debug events'}, {'sha': '4d0ed40d220e15f93ed2e308562ce48e09b69d89', 'message': 'feedback updates'}]
review_comments:
['`resolvePendingExpansions` now emits an edge where `fromY` (bottom of merged node) is greater than `toY` (midpoint of the first expanded child). The edge renderer’s orthogonal routing assumes downward flow (`edge.toY - edge.fromY` is used to compute the corner radius), so this can produce a negative corner radius and malformed paths/incorrect arrow direction for expanded merged nodes. Consider keeping expanded-merge edges horizontally aligned (as before) or updating `renderEdges` to correctly handle edges that route down-then-across-then-up (and clamp corner radius to a non-negative value).

See below for a potential fix:
```
		// Keep this horizontally aligned with the first expanded child so
		// the existing orthogonal renderer does not need to route back up.
		const targetMidY = childNodes[0].y + childNodes[0].height / 2;
		result.edges.push({
			fromId: mergedNode.id,
			toId: childNodes[0].id,
			fromX: mergedNode.x + mergedNode.width / 2,
			fromY: targetMidY,
```', 'The per-session `_seenEventIds` map grows monotonically as new unique event IDs arrive, but it’s never pruned when the per-session ring buffer evicts old events (only on `clear()` / session eviction / provider re-invocation). In long-running sessions with lots of unique IDs, this can lead to unbounded memory growth. Consider bounding this structure (e.g., an LRU capped to `MAX_EVENTS_PER_SESSION` or tracking IDs alongside the ring buffer so evicted IDs are removed).', 'New deduplication behavior (skipping duplicates on `addEvent` and filtering by "richest" kind in `getEvents`) isn’t covered by tests, even though this file already has comprehensive unit tests. Please add tests that (1) verify duplicates with the same `id` collapse to a single event in `getEvents(session)`, and (2) verify the priority behavior (e.g., `subagentInvocation` wins over `userMessage`, and `onDidAddEvent` only fires for the retained event when a duplicate arrives).', 'fixed', 'fixed', 'fixed ', "## Pull request overview

This PR updates the chat “Agent Debug Panel” flow chart to correctly render subagent hierarchies after the extension switched from OTEL spans to JSONL-based debug events.

**Changes:**
- Add per-session event de-duplication in `ChatDebugServiceImpl` to handle multiple events sharing the same `id`, preferring the “richer” event kind.
- Update flow-graph construction to keep subagent tool calls for correct parent/child linkage, then collapse/flatten them post-build (and avoid double “Subagent:” prefixing).
- Adjust the expanded-merged-node edge geometry in the flow layout.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatDebugServiceImpl.ts | Adds ID-based dedup tracking + filtering to reduce duplicate debug events per session. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatDebug/chatDebugFlowLayout.ts | Tweaks edge endpoints for expanded merged nodes in the flow layout. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatDebug/chatDebugFlowGraph.ts | Revises subagent/tool-call handling to preserve hierarchy and clean up labeling. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings




- **Files reviewed:** 3/3 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 3


"]

commits: [{'sha': '3998008785e73acf24deb02e35e3c16d9afab71e', 'message': 'Add more telemetry for chat model retainers'}]
review_comments:
['`ChatModelCreatedEvent` types `newModelLocation` as `string`, but the value being logged is a `ChatAgentLocation` enum. Consider typing it as `ChatAgentLocation` (and keeping the classification comment) to prevent accidentally logging unexpected/free-form strings in the future and to keep the event schema self-documenting.
```suggestion
	newModelLocation: ChatAgentLocation;
```', "## Pull request overview

This PR expands chat telemetry to better understand how many chat models are retained in the background and why, by logging richer model-count snapshots at startup and when new chat models are created.

**Changes:**
- Added a new `ChatModelCountTelemetry` workbench contribution that logs a detailed model snapshot at startup and on subsequent model creations.
- Categorized background models by specific known retainers (`modifiedEditsKeepAlive`, `requestInProgressKeepAlive`) vs “other holders”.
- Moved/remodeled the prior “models at startup” telemetry out of `chat.contribution.ts` into a dedicated telemetry module.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/telemetry/chatModelCountTelemetry.ts` | New telemetry contribution that logs startup and per-model-creation model/retainer counts. |
| `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chat.contribution.ts` | Imports the new telemetry module for registration and removes the previous inline startup telemetry contribution/registration. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings




- **Files reviewed:** 2/2 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 1


"]

commits: [{'sha': 'a3643a5d22a7caa2d1fc4b8e6635552a4c7689d1', 'message': 'less thikning layout thrashing - cache thinking heights'}]
review_comments:
['The `_onDidChangeHeight` handler returns early during streaming when the ResizeObserver is active, so it only updates the cached `lastKnownContentHeight` but never calls `syncDimensionsAndScheduleScroll()` / `updateScrollDimensionsFromCache()`. This contradicts the comment about falling back once the wrapper hits `max-height` (when ResizeObserver may stop firing), and can leave scroll dimensions/autoscroll stale while content keeps streaming.

Consider updating dimensions after `refreshContentHeight()` when `lastKnownContentHeight` exceeds `THINKING_SCROLL_MAX_HEIGHT` (or simply calling `syncDimensionsAndScheduleScroll()` here).', '`chat-list-at-bottom` is only toggled from `onDidScroll` and `setScrollLock()`. Since the scroll-down button starts hidden and `updateScrollDownButtonVisibility()` isn’t called during initialization, the container may never get `chat-list-at-bottom` on first render even though `_scrollLock` defaults to `true`.

If the new CSS relies on this class to reserve space at the bottom during streaming, call `updateScrollDownButtonVisibility()` once after the tree/button are created (or after the first `setChildren`/layout) so initial state is correct.', 'The new rule hardcodes `padding-bottom: 37px`, which is a brittle magic number and may not scale well with zoom/font-size changes. Consider deriving this from existing progress widget sizing (e.g., via a CSS custom property or `calc()` based on line-height/padding), or add a short comment explaining what 37px corresponds to so it can be kept in sync if the progress UI changes.', "## Pull request overview

This PR targets chat widget layout stability and performance during streaming by reducing repeated DOM layout reads in the “thinking” UI and by reserving space to avoid response height jumps when progress UI (e.g. shimmer) appears/disappears.

**Changes:**

- Add a `chat-list-at-bottom` state class on the list container and a `chat-progress-reservable` state class on streaming responses.
- Reserve extra bottom space via CSS when the list is at the bottom to reduce height jank as progress UI toggles.

commits:
[{'sha': 'e1afa1215546acc9f4cb8ecc3946a72add328f71', 'message': "Detect extension-sourced items in provider path for correct grouping

Items from VS Code extensions (under extensions/ directory) were
classified as 'user' storage because _inferStorageAndGroup only
checked workspace folders, plugins, and file: scheme. Now detects
extension install directories by finding 'extensions' in the path
segments and extracting the extension ID from the folder name.
Items from the default chat extension are classified as Built-in;
items from other extensions as Extensions.

This aligns the provider path grouping with how the core (Local)
path handles extension-contributed skills, agents, and other items.

Fixes microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#7330"}, {'sha': 'b726e387195756714e94532e28a3a3c6b20143b3', 'message': "Make built-in and extension items read-only in provider path

Set storage alongside groupKey in _inferStorageAndGroup so that
built-in items (non-file schemes, chat extension) always have
PromptsStorage.extension as their storage. This ensures the
delete action's when-clause correctly hides the trash icon for
read-only items without needing a groupKey fallback hack.

Fixes microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog#7330"}]
review_comments:
['The new extension-install-path inference (splitting `uri.path` and parsing `<id>-<version>`) is subtle and easy to regress (different OS paths, built-in vs non-built-in extension IDs, version formats). Please add unit coverage for this inference (ideally by extracting it into a testable helper) so we verify: (1) extension items map to `PromptsStorage.extension`, (2) default chat extension items land in the Built-in group, and (3) non-extension paths still fall back to `PromptsStorage.user`.

This issue also appears on line 1798 of the same file.', "## Pull request overview

This PR fixes mis-grouping of extension-contributed Chat Customizations when using an external provider (e.g. Copilot CLI) by inferring “Extensions” vs “Built-in” from `file:` URIs that point into the local extensions install directory.

**Changes:**
- Extend `_inferStorageAndGroup` to detect `.../extensions/<extId>-<version>/...` and classify items as `PromptsStorage.extension`.
- Special-case the default chat extension to group its items under **Built-in** instead of **Extensions**.

<details open>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts | Adds extension-install-directory detection to provider-path storage inference to group extension-sourced items correctly. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/aiCustomizationListWidget.ts:1803**
* In the built-in extension case, `_inferStorageAndGroup` returns only `{ groupKey: BUILTIN_STORAGE }` and leaves `storage` undefined. In the provider path that means `AI_CUSTOMIZATION_ITEM_STORAGE_KEY` is not set for these items, so context-menu when-clauses (e.g. deletable checks) will treat them like normal provider items and may incorrectly allow actions like Delete/Reveal/Edit. Keep `storage` as `PromptsStorage.extension` while overriding grouping via `groupKey` (and/or set an explicit read-only storage key) so built-in items remain correctly treated as extension-sourced/read-only.
```
			const versionMatch = folderName.match(/^(.+)-\d+\./);
			const extensionId = versionMatch ? versionMatch[1] : folderName;
			if (this.isChatExtensionItem(new ExtensionIdentifier(extensionId))) {
				return { groupKey: BUILTIN_STORAGE };
			}
			return { storage: PromptsStorage.extension };
```
</details>


- **Files reviewed:** 1/1 changed files
- **Comments generated:** 1


"]

[{'sha': '9cc500e31576e995eae4809fe8fbe5f262517c7d', 'message': 'Remove startup revive of chat sessions with pending edits

Remove reviveSessionsWithEdits() and the whenSessionsRevived property from
IChatService. This code eagerly loaded sessions at startup if they had
pending edits, which is no longer needed.

Also removes the modelsKeptAliveOnlyForEdits telemetry metric that was
specific to this revive path. The hasPendingEdits field is kept on the
serialized data and storage index for potential future use.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>'}]
review_comments:
["## Pull request overview

Removes the chat startup code path that eagerly revived chat sessions solely to restore pending edits, and cleans up the related `IChatService` API surface and telemetry, while keeping `hasPendingEdits` in persisted data for potential future use.

**Changes:**
- Removed `reviveSessionsWithEdits()` and `whenSessionsRevived` from `IChatService`/`ChatService`, along with the corresponding mock implementation.
- Simplified the serialized data comment for `hasPendingEdits` while retaining the field in the v3 serialized shape.
- Updated startup telemetry to no longer wait for session revival and removed the `modelsKeptAliveOnlyForEdits` metric.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |

| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/model/chatModel.ts | Keeps `hasPendingEdits` in serialized v3 data and simplifies its documentation comment. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts | Removes the startup revive implementation and the `whenSessionsRevived` property initialization. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatService.ts | Removes `whenSessionsRevived` from the `IChatService` interface. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chat.contribution.ts | Removes dependency on `whenSessionsRevived` and drops the `modelsKeptAliveOnlyForEdits` telemetry field/classification. |
</details>

## Copilot's findings

- **Comments generated:** 0


"]

commits:
[{'sha': '19b96bbde958b437f342b35319616e02b68287d0', 'message': 'Initial plan'}, {'sha': 'e8d93f502fe62f2c6a5c021e72cce258c00ef08c', 'message': 'Add responsive compact layout for agent sessions web view

When the viewport width falls below 600px, the sessions workbench
switches to a compact single-column layout:
- Sidebar and auxiliary bar are auto-hidden
- Card margins, border-radius and side borders are removed for full-bleed
- Chat content and input padding is tightened
- Part appear transitions are disabled
- Titlebar padding is reduced

Compact mode is detected early in initLayout() so the initial grid
descriptor uses the correct part visibility, and dynamically in layout()
to handle viewport resizing.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/sessions/22feabbb-79bd-4057-b7f9-7c5456de92b1

Co-authored-by: osortega <48293249+osortega@users.noreply.github.com>'}]
review_comments:
['@copilot are these changes scoped to only when running in web AND responsiveness only for small screens like mobile.
In other words, running in web on a machine that has a big enough viewport should still look the same as it does today', "
> @copilot are these changes scoped to only when running in web AND responsiveness only for small screens like mobile.
> In other words, running in web on a machine that has a big enough viewport should s...

Yes, a big viewport looks identical to today. Every CSS rule is gated behind `.agent-sessions-workbench.compact`, and the `.compact` class is only added when the viewport width drops below 600px. On any viewport ≥ 600px, no compact rules apply and no layout changes occur.

The JS logic also only fires on state transitions — it doesn't run on every resize, only when crossing the 600px threshold. When entering compact mode it auto-hides the sidebar and auxiliary bar; when leaving compact mode those parts remain in whatever state the user left them (they don't force-show back).

Note: the changes apply to the sessions workbench on both web and desktop, which is intentional — if someone resizes the desktop sessions window very narrow, the layout should still adapt gracefully. But on a typical desktop viewport this never triggers."]
